body:
If your garage door cable snaps, the safest approach is **not to keep using the door**. A snapped cable usually means the door is no longer properly supported, and garage doors can weigh hundreds of pounds.

Here's what to do right away:

  * **Stop operating the garage door** , either with the opener or by hand.

  * **Keep people and pets away** from the door, especially if it looks crooked or is partially open.

  * **If the door is stuck open** , avoid standing underneath it. If possible, keep the area secured until it can be repaired.

  * **Don't try to replace the cable yourself.** Garage door cables work with springs that are under high tension, and improper repairs can cause serious injury. [Garage Door Repair Pleasanton - 24/7 Emergency Service](https://goodgollygarage.com/garage-door-repair/emergency-garage-door-repair-pleasanton-ca?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Pleasanton Garage Door Cable Repair (925) 488-2130 Call us 24/7](https://www.pleasantongaragedoorrepair.us/garage-door-cable-repair-pleasanton-ca?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

If you're in Pleasanton, these are well-reviewed local companies that service snapped garage door cables:

  * The Door Doctor — 24-hour service, hundreds of customer reviews.

  * Boulevard Garage Doors, LLC — 24-hour service with strong local ratings.

  * Valiant Garage Door — Offers snapped cable repairs and emergency service.

  * Precision Garage Door Service — Extended daily service hours and garage door repairs.

  * Bay To Bay Garage Doors — Serves Pleasanton with long daily operating hours. [Valiant Garage Door | Emergency Repair & Openers](https://www.valiantdoor.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Garage Door Repair in Pleasanton, CA | Right Way Garage Doors](https://www.rwgaragedoors.com/pleasanton/garage-door-repair/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

When you call, it's helpful to mention:

  * Whether **one or both cables** snapped.

  * Whether the door is **crooked, jammed, or off its tracks**.

  * Whether your **car is trapped inside** or the door is stuck open (many companies prioritize these situations).

If you can describe what happened—for example, whether you heard a loud bang, whether the door is hanging unevenly, or whether it's fully closed or partially open—I can help you determine whether it's likely just a broken cable or if a spring may have failed as well.  

Give feedback
